name: Conjunctive System (Parallel Composition)
description: Definition of the conjunctive system CSY(V) of a connectable vector of systems V, including state/input/output product spaces, next state/readout functions, and port mapping relations.

elements.text: CSY = {(V, Z): V is a connectable vector of systems; if V = Z1, then Z = Z1; if V = (Z1, ..., Zn), then Z = (SZ, IZ, OZ, NZ, RZ).
NZ = {((x, p), y) : x ∈ SZ; p ∈ IZ; y ∈ SZ; if i ∈ IJS[1, n], then PJNi(y) = NZi(PJNi(x), PJNPZi(p))}.
RZ = {(x, q): x ∈ SZ; q ∈ OZ; if i ∈ IJS[1, n], then PJNOPZi(q) = RZi(PJNi(x))}.
IP& is a function mapping the input ports of the conjunctive system to the input ports of the component systems.
INIP& is the inverse function of IP&.
IS& is the input port structure function of the conjunctive system.
OP& is a function mapping the output ports of the conjunctive system to the output ports of the component systems.
INOP& is the inverse function of OP&.
OS& is the output port structure function of the conjunctive system.
